items.title: School denies parents' claims of how AI deepfake scandal was handled

children.content: The principal of a Tasmanian private school denies claims by two parents that the school ever tried to discourage them from telling their daughters they had been identified in AI deepfake images.

items.title: Music promoter charged with cocaine, cannabis offences

children.content: The former managing director of Hobart events company Vandemonian Touring appears in court charged with trafficking cocaine and possessing cannabis.

image.altText: A crowd faces away from the camera and towards a stage where Bliss n Eso are performing

items.title: ASIC drops case against Liberty Bell Bay smelter as buyer sought

children.content: It was alleged Liberty Bell Bay's former owner, British billionaire Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance, had failed to lodge financial returns for numerous years. The company then went into administration, and ASIC has dropped its legal case.

image.altText: A blue sign with a flame on it and the word 'LIBERTY' underneath, on the side of the road.

items.title: 'Gobsmacking': How a council's push to work four days a week came unstuck

children.content: It wanted to be the first Australian council to usher in a four-day work week. Documents show there was never a standalone business case to back the proposal — but there was a tight grip on messaging.

image.altText: a man in a suit with cheque tie smiles at the camera

items.title: Launceston businesses 'not convinced' Devils will be a better deal than Hawks

children.content: The Tasmanian government has indicated the deal with Hawthorn to play games in Launceston will end next year, as the Tasmania Devils prepare to enter the AFL in 2028.

image.altText: Aerial view of a stadium.

items.title: Israel’s massive strike was opposite of surgical, Lebanon deputy PM says

children.content: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

items.title: War deepens oil crisis and pushes inflation higher — as it happened

children.content: The price of Brent crude has surged to over $US120 per barrel because US President Donald Trump has announced he will extend the naval blockade of the strait.

image.altText: Several large tankers and ships in an open ocean.

items.title: 'It's a safety issue': Construction industry faces gambling crisis

children.content: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

image.altText: Men in high-viz and hard hats stand around building supplied chatting.

items.title: Has the world's most powerful cartel met its end?

children.content: A United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for 66 years.

articles.title: ASX falls as oil price hits four-year high of $US126

children.content: The Australian share market is trading lower as
the oil price surges above $US125 in the afternoon trading. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

articles.title: Australia's summer saw the dawn of the big battery, as gas drops to 25-year low

children.content: A hot summer saw Australians use record amounts of electricity, but the growing share of renewable energy continued to push fossil fuels out of the grid, driving gas generation to its lowest level in 25 years.

items.title: Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k to MP he sexually harassed and vilified

children.content: The former One Nation MP has been found to have vilified openly gay politician Alex Greenwich in the second case centring around a dispute between the pair.

image.altText: a composite image of Alex Greenwich and Mark Latham

items.title: Why slick home appliances are actually harder for some to use

children.content: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

items.title: Artist who taught himself to paint after seeing Roy and HG portrait wins prize

children.content: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

items.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother to remain in custody

children.content: The mother of former police officer Beau Lamarre-Condon will spend at least two weeks in custody after she was charged over an alleged attempt to influence a key witness to change their evidence in his upcoming murder trial.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt smiling next to woman in white top

items.title: Piastri ready to race again in Miami after F1's enforced break

children.content: Given a month-long enforced break, Australia's Formula 1 star Oscar Piastri is keen to regain lost momentum when racing resumes in Miami this weekend.

items.title: Gifted, goofy, gone too soon: Jeff Buckley's genius remembered

children.content: The otherworldly singer-guitarist died at 30 and struggled with fame. His guitarist and a new movie honour Buckley's humanity and musical genius.

items.title: ATSB confirms student and instructor killed in Parafield plane crash

children.content: The ATSB said the plane had been doing circuit training at the airport, but it's not yet known if structured training was underway when it crashed.

items.title: 'Waiting for me to die': Pension stripped from man with disability

children.content: Ray Noakes was put on the disability pension after he was deemed unable to work. Then he was put on Jobseeker, which he lost because he could not work. Now he wants compensation.

items.title: Why insurers may start offering discounts for resilience renovations

children.content: As about 1.6 million households experience insurance unaffordability stress, a disparate alliance of stakeholders proposes cheaper premiums for home owners who upgrade their properties to withstand natural disasters.

items.title: Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraiser and to catch Florida jet

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

children.content: A rail equipment fault caused havoc on the V/Line Geelong Line on Wednesday night, with rail operators warning of more delays today.

items.title: Cases of potentially deadly disease triple in the NT

children.content: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

children.content: Anglicare Australia's latest look at rentals shows the private market effectively does not exist for poorer Australians.

items.title: 'Free Palestine' party plans to funnel votes to Victorian conservatives
